IIT Bombay Latest Updates and News

30 Jun, 2026 IIT Bombay JEE Advanced Cutoff 2026 has been released. Check round and course-wise IIT Bombay JEE Advanced Cutoff 2026.

  • Round 2 Cutoff for B.Tech is 65-5137 for the General Category.
...Read More

IIT Bombay cutoff is available for JEE-Advanced, IIT-JAM, GATE, UCEED, CAT here. You can check category wise IIT Bombay cutoff for popular courses such as B.Tech, B.Tech + M.Tech, BS, M.Sc, M.Sc + Ph.D, M.Tech, B.Des, MBA below.

IIT Bombay offers admission to B.Tech through JEE-Advanced. For Admission in B.Tech + M.Tech courses, IIT Bombay accept JEE-Advanced score. You can check the table below to know the accepted exams for courses offered by IIT Bombay (Cutoff Range below is across all categories).

Course Exam Cutoff
B.Tech JEE-Advanced 1 - 5137
B.Tech + M.Tech JEE-Advanced 8 - 844
BS JEE-Advanced 16 - 7360
M.Sc IIT-JAM 16 - 4970
M.Tech GATE 217 - 675
B.Des UCEED 1 - 15
MBA CAT 60 - 90

IIT Bombay JEE-Advanced Cutoff 2026

Students can take admission in B.Tech, B.Tech + M.Tech, BS programs at IIT Bombay based on JEE-Advanced score. As per IIT Bombay JEE-Advanced Cutoff 2026, the overall cutoff for B.Tech programs in Round 2 is 1 - 5137 across all categories.

  • For B.Tech + M.Tech, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 8 - 844 across all categories.
  • For BS, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 16 - 7360 across all categories.

B.Tech Computer Science and Engineering, B.Tech + M.Tech Electrical Engineering, BS Mathematics and Scientific Computing, are the top choices for students appearing in JEE-Advanced.

In 2026, B.Tech Computer Science and Engineering closing rank for General Category is 65 in Round 2.

  • For B.Tech + M.Tech Electrical Engineering, The JEE-Advanced closing rank 2026 for General is 844 in Round 2.
  • For BS Mathematics and Scientific Computing, The JEE-Advanced closing rank 2026 for General is 1042 in Round 2.

You can check B.Tech Computer Science and Engineering, B.Tech + M.Tech Electrical Engineering, BS Mathematics and Scientific Computing, category-wise closing rank for JEE-Advanced below:

Cutoff Summary: IIT Bombay

  • GATE
  • JEE Advanced
  • IIT JAM
  • UCEED

The GATE 2025 cutoff for admission to M.Tech programs at IIT Bombay in the General category ranges from 750 to 325. For admission to M.Tech Computer Science and Engineering, the cutoff score is 750, and the cutoff for M.Tech Structural Engineering is 675. Other competitive programs such as Transportation Engineering, Industrial Engineering and Operations Research, and Design Engineering have admission cutoffs in the range of 650 to 600. Courses like M.Tech Computational Materials Engineering, Geoinformatics & Natural Resources Engineering, and Geoexploration show cutoffs between 400 and 325.

For OBC candidates, the cutoff score is 675 for Computer Science and Engineering and 293 for Petroleum Geoscience. In the SC category, the score for Computer Science and Engineering is 500, and for Geoexploration, it is 217. For ST candidates, the cutoff for Computer Science and Engineering is 500, while the score for Geoexploration is 217. Under the EWS category, the admission cutoff is 750 for Computer Science and Engineering and 325 for Petroleum Geoscience.

...Read More
 By Collegedunia’s Personal AI

Can I get into IIT Bombay with Rank 3000 in JEE Advanced?

Ambika Das

3000 JEE Advanced rank for IIT Bombay — be realistic about this:

IIT Bombay is the most competitive IIT and at 3000 general rank, most flagship programs are beyond reach:

  • CSE at IIT Bombay typically closes around rank 50-100 for general. far out of reach at 3000
  • EE, Mech, Chemical, Civil — all close in the 300-1200 range for general. not accessible at 3000
  • what might open at IIT Bombay at 3000 general: Energy Science & Engineering, Metallurgical, some interdisciplinary programs in later rounds. these close in the 2,500–4,000+ range

realistic options at 3000 JEE Advanced general:

  • IIT Delhi — Maths & Computing, Engineering Physics might just open
  • IIT Roorkee — ECE, EE, Chemical available
  • IIT Kharagpur — several branches open
  • IIT Guwahati — CSE, ECE available

to get IIT Bombay's main branches at 3000 rank you'd need to be OBC (category rank ~900-1200 for CSE) or SC/ST. if you're general category, check JoSAA closing ranks from 2024 for IIT Bombay specifically — you might get some interdisciplinary programs but not core engineering. 3000 general is IIT Roorkee/Kharagpur level for good branches, not IIT Bombay mainstream.

...Read More
0
0

Is 12,000 JEE Advanced Rank decent enough for admission into an IIT?

Santosh Kumar

yes, 12,000 JEE Advanced rank is enough to get into an IIT — but branch and campus choices are limited. total IIT seats are 17,385+ so you're well within range.

what's realistic at 12,000 AIR (general category):

  • newer IITs (IIT Bhilai, Goa, Jammu, Dharwad, Tirupati, Palakkad) — can get CSE, ECE, or EE at most of these. these campuses are 6-10 years old, infrastructure is still developing but faculty quality is solid
  • older IITs (BHU, Dhanbad/ISM) — non-core branches like Chemical, Metallurgy, Mining, Bioengineering might open up. CSE/ECE won't
  • IIT Bombay, Delhi, Madras, Kanpur, Kharagpur, Roorkee, Guwahati — basically not happening at 12,000 general

important thing most people miss — your category rank matters more than AIR. if you're OBC/SC/ST, a 12,000 AIR can unlock significantly better IITs and branches. check your category rank separately. also, newer IITs like Bhilai and Goa have been steadily improving placements and research output — don't write them off just because they're newer.

...Read More
0
0

A friend of mine got CSE at IIT Bombay with a rank of 10k. How is it justified?

Tarun Ghosh

The "10K rank getting CSE at IIT Bombay" claim — this is almost certainly not CSE at IIT Bombay main campus. Let me explain what's actually possible at rank 10K.

CSE at IIT Bombay closing rank (JEE Advanced 2024, General category):

  • IIT Bombay CSE closing rank: approximately 50-80 (yes, double digits) in the general category by final round. This is among the most competitive allocations in the world.
  • No 10K rank gets IIT Bombay CS/IT in any category under general.

What a 10K rank could actually get at IIT Bombay:

  • Absolutely NOT CSE main program.
  • Possibly: Engineering Physics, Metallurgical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Earth Sciences — these have closing ranks in the 5,000-12,000 range.
  • Or: 5-year Integrated M.Sc programs at IIT Bombay in some sciences.

What a 10K rank CAN get overall:

  • Good branches at IIT Roorkee, IIT Kharagpur, IIT BHU, IIT Guwahati.
  • CSE at lower IITs (IIT Patna, IIT Mandi, etc.).

So if your friend genuinely got CSE at IIT Bombay with 10K rank: Either the information is inaccurate, they're referring to a different IIT, OR this was under a specific reserved category (SC/ST where cutoffs are very different) or a different program path (like postgraduate/lateral). Ask for clarification — this doesn't add up for regular JEE Advanced general category.

What is the JEE Advanced cutoff trend for admission into IIT Bombay as an OBC candidate?

Krutika SenBTech (2022-Present), IIT Bombay

I have listed below the JEE Advanced 2023 cutoffs for IIT Bombay for the OBC NCL category for your reference:

IIT Bombay BTech Cutoff 2023 for OBC NCL 

IIT Bombay BTech specialisations JEE Advanced Cut-off 2023 for OBC NCL
BTech Computer Science Engineering 58
BTech Electrical Engineering 349
BTech+MTech Electrical Engineering 674
BTech Mechanical Engineering 942
BTech Chemical Engineering 1368
BTech Aerospace Engineering 1499
BTech Energy Engineering 1811
BTech Civil Engineering 1884
BTech Metallurgical and Materials Engineering 2284
BTech Environmental Engineering 2761

I hope the previous year's cutoff will give you an idea of the general trend followed by IIT Bombay for admissions of OBC NCL candidates through JEE Advanced.

Can I get into IIT Bombay with a rank of 600 in JEE Advanced?

RiteshBTech (2022-Present), IIT Bombay

Yes, you have a fair chance to get into most BTech courses at IIT Bombay with this rank. To understand better, take a look at last year’s IIT Bombay cutoffs for the General category:

IIT Bombay BTech Cutoff 2023

IIT Bombay BTech specialisations JEE Advanced Cut-off 2023
BTech Computer Science Engineering 66
BTech Electrical Engineering 463
BTech+MTech Electrical Engineering 966
BTech Engineering Physics 1125
BTech Mechanical Engineering 1691
BTech Aerospace Engineering 2568
BTech Chemical Engineering 2605
BTech Energy Engineering 2694
BTech Civil Engineering 4134
BTech Metallurgical and Materials Engineering 4428
BTech Environmental Engineering 5221

Since your rank is 600 in JEE Advanced, you can expect admission to almost every B.Tech specialisation of IIT Bombay this year (except CSE and Electrical).

What are the GATE ranks for M.Tech courses at IIT Bombay?

Ronit SharmaLives in Allahabad, Uttar Pradesh, India

Since my sister was a student of Civil Engineering branch at IIT Bombay, I have information about the same only.

  • Structural Engineering: 1–250
  • Geotechnical Engineering: 250–450
  • Transportation Engineering: 400-550
  • Water Resources Engineering: 500
  • Ocean Engineering: don’t remember

The above ranks are for General Category only. Reserved categories can expect a call at a rank higher than that mentioned above. The overall admission procedure comprises 75% weightage to GATE score and 25% weightage to written test and interview.

0

What rank is required in JEE Advanced to get CSE in IIT Bombay?

Pratik GandhiB.Tech. CSE, IIT Bombay

To get CSE in IIT Bombay, you need a JEE Advanced rank of 60 if you belong to General Category. This rank keeps getting lower every year. If you are certain of studying CSE at IIT Bombay, then start studying without further hesitation. In case you are still not sure, I have a friend's experience that might help you make the decision. He was interested in Mechanical and was not sure about CSE. With a rank of 70, he went for Mechanical Engineering. 

Now there is a course at IIT Bombay that is compulsory for all freshers. The course comprises the fundamentals of Computer Science. Once he studied that, he became certain that he wanted to pursue CSE instead and requested a branch change. In fact, I know many who changed their branch after becoming aware of their passion and inclinations. So you don’t have to necessarily make a decision right now, and can always avail of the branch change option later on. But make sure to make an early decision. Also if your JEE rank is average then you can always change your branch later on. However, an important thing to keep in mind is that this branch change option is possible only if your first-year CGPA is up to the mark.

To get a seat in IIT Bombay in computer science, how many marks should a person make on the IIT Advance?

Pratik GandhiB.Tech. CSE, IIT Bombay

To get a seat at IIT Bombay in computer science, one must qualify in JEE Advanced and obtain a high rank. JEE Advanced is the entrance exam for admission to the IITs. The cutoff marks for JEE Advanced vary each year based on various factors such as the difficulty level of the exam, the number of applicants, and the number of available seats. 

In general, a candidate must score well above the minimum cutoff marks to be considered for admission to IIT Bombay. In recent years, those who have secured a rank between 1 and 100 have had a good chance of getting admission to CS in IIT Bombay. However, this is not a fixed criterion and may vary based on the year and the specific circumstances.

What rank should we get to enter the IIT Bombay?

Amogh WaghB.Tech in Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay (IITB)

 It's important to note that when considering the undergraduate program at IIT Bombay, you should focus solely on the general category ranks listed below. 

For the 4-year B-Tech course, you will need to keep in mind the following ranks:

  • Computer Science Engineering ~ AIR (2 - 63)
  • Electrical Engineering ~ AIR (25 - 288)
  • Mechanical Engineering ~ AIR (291 - 1110)
  • Engineering Physics ~ AIR (309 - 1101)
  • Aerospace Engineering ~ AIR (807 - 2070)
  • Chemical Engineering ~ AIR (832 - 1922)
  • Civil Engineering ~ AIR (1498 - 3267)
  • Metallurgical Engineering and Materials Science ~ AIR (2229 - 3574)

For the 4-year B.S course, the ranks are as follows:

  • Mathematics ~ AIR (128 - 1511)
  • Economics ~ AIR (819 - 2176)
  • Chemistry ~ AIR (2541 - 5700)

For the 5-Year B.Tech + M.Tech. (Dual Degree) Course, the ranks are as follows:

  • Electrical Engineering ~ AIR (301 - 749)
  • Energy Engineering with M.Tech in Energy Systems Engineering ~ AIR (1566 - 2469)
  • Environmental Science and Engineering ~ AIR (3830 - 5209)
  • Mechanical Engineering and M.Tech in Computer Integrated Manufacturing ~ AIR (1195 - 1571)
  • Metallurgical Engineering & Materials Science ~ AIR (3688 - 4213)

Remember that these statistics are important to keep in mind when applying to IIT Bombay.
